channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Rhode Island woman works 350 hours to build first home
item.description: "It makes you excited to think that you're going to build your own home and help others to get theirs," she told NewsNatoin affiliate WPRI. "That's like a dream come true.
item.dc:creator: Catie McNeil, NewsNation

item.title: Los Angeles prosecutors to review new evidence in Menendez brothers' 1996 murder conviction
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Prosecutors in Los Angeles are reviewing new evidence in the case of Erik and Lyle Menendez to determine whether they should be serving life sentences for killing their parents in their Beverly Hills mansion more than 35 years ago, the city's district attorney said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Navy veteran student recounts saving his ASU classmate from stabbing attack: 'Compelled to do something'
item.description: An Arizona State University student sprang into action to help classmate during "random" stabbing attack on the campus, saying that he felt "compelled" to help.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-NYPD boss rejected discipline for cops who raided bar where owner says he was 'extorted'
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In late August, less than a week before federal agents arrived at his home with a search warrant, New York Police Department Commissioner Edward Caban cleared three officers found to have engaged in misconduct during a raid on a Brooklyn bar.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dockworkers' union to suspend strike until Jan. 15
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports has reached a deal to suspend their strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract, a person briefed on the matter says.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 3 ex-officers convicted in fatal beating of Tyre Nichols
item.description: Former Memphis police officers Tadarrius Bean, Demetrius Haley and Justin Smith were convicted Thursday in the fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, but cleared of the most serious charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Subway rider shot in the head by police files claim accusing officers of recklessly opening fire
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A lawyer for a New York City subway rider who was inadvertently shot in the head by police at a station has filed a legal claim against the city, accusing the officers of showing “carelessness and reckless” disregard for the lives of others when they opened fire on a man holding
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 3 officers convicted in Tyre Nichols fatal beating, 2 acquitted of civil rights charges
item.description: Three former Memphis officers were convicted Thursday of charges of witness tampering in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, and two were acquitted of federal civil rights violations in a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Los Angeles prosecutors to review new evidence in Menendez brothers' 1996 murder conviction
item.description: Prosecutors in Los Angeles say they are reviewing new evidence in the case of the Menendez brothers to determine whether they should be serving life sentences for killing their parents in their Beverly Hills mansion more than 35 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Country music star Garth Brooks accused of sexual assault by makeup artist
item.description: Garth Brooks, the country music superstar who gained fame in the 1990s with several hit songs, has been named in a lawsuit accusing him of raping a hair and makeup artist in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden's student loan cancellation is put on hold again after day of legal whiplash
item.description: A federal judge in Missouri put a temporary hold on President Joe Biden’s latest student loan cancellation plan on Thursday, slamming the door on hope it would move forward after another judge allowed a pause to expire.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Blue Ridge Parkway to be closed indefinitely, National Park Service says
item.description: It comes right as the region – and road – typically sees a boom in usage as weather cools and the leaves change colors.
item.dc:creator: Doug Coats, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Melania Trump says she supports abortion rights, putting her at odds with the GOP
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Melania Trump revealed her support for abortion rights Thursday ahead of the release of her upcoming memoir, exposing a stark contrast with her husband, Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump, on the crucial election issue.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Biden student loan forgiveness plan gets win in Georgia court
item.description: President Joe Biden’s student debt relief plan was dealt a win by a Georgia judge this week by removing one Republican challenger and letting the program advance for now after a temporarily restraining order expires.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bipartisan California lawmakers urge House leaders to boost disaster relief funding
item.description: A bipartisan group of lawmakers from Orange County, Calif., are urging House leaders to include disaster relief funding in fiscal 2025 spending, highlighting wildfire damage in the state even as damage from Hurricane Helene on the other side of the country has heightened attention on the issue.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Florida law banning homeless people from sleeping in public spaces takes effect
item.description: Under the law, municipalities are required to create designated spaces for camping and sleeping if homeless shelters are full.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How Black leaders in New York are grappling with Eric Adams and representation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It wasn’t a shock to many Black New Yorkers that Mayor Eric Adams has surrounded himself with African American civil rights leaders, clergy and grassroots activists since his indictment last week on federal bribery charges.
item.dc:creator: AARON MORRISON and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: VIDEO: Helene waves and storm surge crash into Florida home as owners watch
item.description: Terrifying moments caught on video showed Helene’s powerful and deadly storm surge as it crashed into a home.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, NewsNation

item.title: Voters in 13 states have less than a week to register. When is the deadline in Illinois?
item.description: The 2024 presidential election is fast approaching, and in some states, the voter registration deadline is as soon as this week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, WGN 9

item.title: Trump downplays troop injuries in 2020 missile strike: 'You mean because they had a headache?'
item.description: Trump was asked whether he should have responded more strongly to Iran after it launched missiles at U.S. forces stationed at a base in Iraq in 2020, leaving dozens with traumatic brain injuries.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Walz on Tiananmen Square screw-up: 'I'm a knucklehead at times'
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), pressed at the vice presidential debate over false statements he made in which he said he was present during the Tiananmen Square protests in 1989, said: “I’m a knucklehead at times.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, Fox 5

item.title: Men accused of killing Jocelyn Nungaray believed to be Venezuelan gang members: search warrant
item.description: Search warrants reveal how investigators have looked into the social media accounts of the men accused of murdering 12-year-old Jocelyn Nungaray.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How you can help after Helene
item.description: Reporters who rode out the storm will explain what Americans faced in the hours after Helene arrived. Red Cross representatives will also explain how your donations are being used to help Americans across 10 states get back on their feet.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: Woman recounts her rescue from river, husband's death
item.description: WASHINGTON COUNTY, Tenn. (WJHL) — Some of the best years of Vicki Hunter's life came to a tragic end Friday after a raging Nolichucky River ripped through the bottomland where she and her husband, Jerry, lived with more than a dozen horses.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Keeling, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Melania Trump: ‘We are dealing with much more danger’
item.description: Former first lady Melania Trump weighed in on the state of former President Donald Trump's bid for the White House in an interview Wednesday, while also reflecting on the recent assassination attempts on her husband's life.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bought a Clif Bar? You could be paid as part of a class action settlement
item.description: A lawsuit against Clif Bar & Company alleged that Clif Bars and Clif Kid ZBars were unhealthy due to added sugars, despite being advertised with slogans like "Nutrition for Sustained Energy" and "No High Fructose Corn Syrup" on the product's packaging.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Irate residents halt construction of migrant shelter
item.description: Residents of a southeast Juarez neighborhood are demanding the government call off plans to build a migrant shelter near their children’s schools.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Florida communities hit three times by hurricanes grapple with how and whether to rebuild
item.description: HORSESHOE BEACH, Fla. (AP) — It was just a month ago that Brooke Hiers left the state-issued emergency trailer where her family had lived since Hurricane Idalia slammed into her Gulf Coast fishing village of Horseshoe Beach in August 2023.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and DAVID R. MARTIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Last pandemic-era student loan benefit ends even as borrowers struggle to make payments
item.description: The last of the pandemic-era student loan benefits are gone, winding down four years of additional help to borrowers that advocates say was never actually enough.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Helene scrambles politics in 3 battleground states
item.description: The 500-mile-long path of destruction cut by Hurricane Helene has scrambled the politics of three battleground states that could determine control of the White House and Senate: North Carolina, Georgia and Florida.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Costco now selling platinum bars and coins on website
item.description: Following the smash success of adding gold and silver bars and coins to its list of products, Costco has added a new precious metal to its stores: platinum.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Parents spot kids in 7-Eleven robbery footage, turn them in to police
item.description: In each instance, the crew rolled up to the stores on bicycles during the evening hours and stole items, damaged property and left without paying, police said.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: Trump rejects calls for another debate with Harris after VP showdown
item.description: Former President Donald Trump again rejected the prospect of another debate with Vice President Kamala Harris late Tuesday following a showdown between the two vice presidential nominees, saying he was "not looking to do it again.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: Push to map Great Lakes bottom gains momentum amid promises effort will help fishing and shipping
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Jennifer Boehme grew up scouting beaches around her home in St. Petersburg, Florida, for whatever she could find. Rocks, sand dollars, coquina mollusks — anything the ocean gave up. Now, 40 years later, Boehme wants to launch another treasure hunt.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justice Department finds Georgia is 'deliberately indifferent' to unchecked abuses at its prisons
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia prison officials are “deliberately indifferent” to unchecked deadly violence, widespread drug use, extortion and sexual abuse at state lockups, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BY JEFF MARTIN, KEVIN McGILL and ALANNA DURKIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Vance called Harris a 'border czar' during the VP debate. What does that mean?
item.description: Republicans, including former President Donald Trump and his 2024 running mate, Senator J.D. Vance of Ohio, have increasingly referred to Vice President Kamala Harris as a failed "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KRON4

item.title: Federal prosecutors file new indictment against ex-Louisville police officers
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Federal prosecutors filed a new indictment Tuesday against two former Louisville officers accused of falsifying a warrant that led police to Breonna Taylor’s door before they fatally shot her.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
